summary: Emotional intelligence assessment app featuring a 50-item Likert (1–5) questionnaire, automated scoring across five emotional competencies (Self-awareness, Managing emotions, Motivating oneself, Empathy, Social skill), interpretation of results by score ranges, and surveyor role permissions to download assessment results.
architectural_notes: Define roles: respondent completes assessment; surveyor can access and download results.
Store per-question responses and computed competency totals to support download/export.
Add a deployment/release process to publish the app to a live (production) environment.

existing_features.title: Implement the EI assessment questionnaire UI containing all 50 provided statements, each answered with a 1–5 Likert score, and include the instructions text explaining the 1/3/5 meaning.

Compute automated scoring across the five emotional competencies using the provided item-to-competency grid mapping (Self awareness: 1,6,11,16,21,26,31,36,41,46; Managing emotions: 2,7,12,17,22,27,32,37,42,47; Motivating oneself: 3,8,13,18,23,28,33,38,43,48; Empathy: 4,9,14,19,24,29,34,39,44,49; Social Skill: 5,10,15,20,25,30,35,40,45,50), and calculate per-competency totals (10 items each).

Interpret each competency total using the provided thresholds (35–50 = Strength, 18–34 = Needs attention, 10–17 = Development priority) and present an analysis view showing each competency’s total and category.

Add authenticated access using Internet Identity and implement a 'surveyor' role that has permission to download respondents’ results; include an admin mechanism to manage the surveyor allowlist (e.g., add/remove surveyor principals) within the app.

Provide a surveyor dashboard that allows surveyors to view stored submissions and download results (at minimum CSV and/or JSON) containing respondent principal, timestamp, answers 1–50, competency totals, and interpretation categories.

Apply a cohesive, creative visual theme for the assessment app (colors, typography, layout) and use it consistently across the questionnaire, results view, and surveyor dashboard (avoid blue/purple as primary colors).

Create a production-ready deployment configuration for the existing EI assessment app on the Internet Computer, including building the frontend assets and deploying the Motoko backend canister and frontend canister as a live/production instance.
Add a production readiness checklist and smoke-test flow to confirm the app is functioning correctly immediately after deployment.

new_feature_requests.summary: Build EI assessment questionnaire UI with 50 statements scored 1–5 (Likert scale)
Auto-calculate totals for 5 competencies using the provided item-to-competency grid mapping
Interpret each competency total using thresholds (35–50 strength, 18–34 needs attention, 10–17 development priority) and present analysis to user
Add surveyor role permissions to download respondents’ results (export/download)

Persist each completed assessment submission in the backend, including: respondent identifier (Internet Identity principal), timestamp, answers for items 1–50, computed totals for the five competencies, and the derived interpretation category for each competency.
Deploy the EI assessment app to a live/production environment (“go live” request)

projectName: Emotional Intelligence (EI) Assessment Survey with Scoring Analysis and Surveyor Download Rights
